title: calophyllum subhorizontale
intro: Calophyllum subhorizontale, also known as the small-leafed camphor tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Australia and Asia, characterized by its small, elliptical leaves and fragrant, camphor-like bark. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum subhorizontale include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent to reduce pain and swelling. Calophyllum subhorizontale is often prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
